verdict: Iter2 correctly implements all three declared fixes (Fix 1: 3-candidate task_id lookup, Fix 2: targetless wildcard semantics, Fix 3: AC3 hard assertion). All live-verified PASS. However two blockers prevent closing: (1) AC4 scope-integrity test fails due to environment contamination from sibling task 20260521-090200 — the test explicitly asserts userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh is absent from git diff HEAD, but sibling uncommitted changes cause it to appear; close debate requires a clean test suite. (2) The posttool task_id mismatch is a confirmed security gap: when a sentinel is keyed by session_id (CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset at grant time), posttool-allowlist-consume.py looks up by data.task_id first and never finds the sentinel, so the sentinel is NOT consumed on the Write PostToolUse event and remains valid until TTL expiry — a single sentinel can authorize multiple Write events within the session. Dev acknowledged this as out_of_scope per dispatch constraint, but dev DID modify posttool-allowlist-consume.py in this cycle. The posttool Write branch is present but incomplete (no candidate-list lookup mirrors write-guard.sh Fix 1). The fix (Fix 1 candidate logic) exists in write-guard.sh but was not replicated to posttool. This is not a pre-existing bug — it is a new architectural gap introduced by this cycle's posttool Write branch that uses a single-candidate lookup while the pretool uses a 3-candidate lookup.

checks.name: Provenance: git diff shows correct files modified
checks.detail: git diff --name-only 1a1d058665a1f525b9e4c219c93f67ebb2ae90bb (baseline_head_sha) shows: hooks/lib/allowlist.py, hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh, hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py, hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py, tests/generated/20260522-080646-B/test_AC3_AC3-posttool-write-sentinel-consume.py. All files declared in dev.files_modified and remediation_2.files_modified are present in diff. hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh appears in working tree diff due to sibling task 20260521-090200 (environment contamination, not caused by this task's dev).

checks.name: Fix 2: allowlist.py targetless wildcard semantics
checks.detail: match_sentinel_grant_for_write() at allowlist.py line 484: entry_target = entry.get('target'); if entry_target is None or entry_target == target_path: return entry. Verified live via Python: (a) sentinel with no 'target' key returns the entry for any path — PASS. (b) sentinel with target=None (explicit null JSON) returns entry — PASS. (c) sentinel with explicit target='/specific/file.py' requires exact match — PASS. (d) sentinel with explicit target='/specific/file.py' does NOT match different path — PASS. iter1 critical failure resolved.

checks.name: Fix 1: pretool-write-guard.sh 3-candidate sentinel lookup
checks.detail: write-guard.sh lines 46-86: builds [writer_key (env_CLAUDE_TASK_ID or session_id), data_task_id, session_id] de-duped candidate list via seen set. Iterates all valid same-session candidates; returns 'match' if any matches, 'valid_no_match' if at least one valid sentinel found but none match, 'none' if no valid sentinel. Verified live: (a) sentinel keyed by session_id, CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset, data.task_id different -> candidate list [session_id, data.task_id] -> sentinel found via session_id -> MATCH PASS. (b) sentinel keyed by CLAUDE_TASK_ID when set -> found via first candidate -> PASS. (c) no sentinel -> 'none' -> PASS. (d) valid sentinel wrong target -> 'valid_no_match' -> PASS. (e) expired sentinel -> not counted as valid -> 'none' -> PASS. CF-1 guard line 94: only fires on 'valid_no_match' AND existing file ([ -f FILE_PATH ]). New-file with mismatched sentinel exits 0 — PASS. iter1 critical failure resolved.

checks.name: TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel check across all 4 AC test files
checks.detail: Grepped all 4 files for literal string 'TEST_INCOMPLETE:'. Zero matches in: test_AC1_AC1-write-sentinel-matcher.py, test_AC2_AC2-write-guard-sentinel-check.py, test_AC3_AC3-posttool-write-sentinel-consume.py, test_AC4_AC4-scope-integrity.py. All test bodies are real implementations.

checks.name: pytest tests/generated/20260522-080646-B/ — AC test results
checks.detail: AC1 PASS (4 match/mismatch unit cases). AC2 PASS (4 write-guard smoke cases: sentinel-match exit 0, CF-1 no-match exit 2, legacy-only exit 0, no-grant exit 2). AC3 PASS (3 posttool consumption cases including hard Bash assertion). AC4 FAIL — test asserts userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh is absent from git diff HEAD but sibling task 20260521-090200 has uncommitted changes to that file in working tree. Primary_cause: environment (not dev_implementation). Failing test blocks clean pytest run.

checks.name: pytest hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py — full suite
checks.detail: 40/40 tests pass. Includes TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite (5 cases: match_returns_entry, session_mismatch_returns_none, target_mismatch_returns_none, expired_sentinel_returns_none, op_mismatch_returns_none). All pass. No regressions in existing test classes.

checks.name: Codex adversarial review of 3-candidate lookup logic and overall verdict
checks.detail: Codex returned 2 BLOCKERs: (1) Posttool task_id mismatch — Write sentinel not consumed when posttool resolves task_id via data.task_id (no candidate list) and sentinel is keyed by session_id. Confirmed live: Write PostToolUse with data.task_id != session_id -> sentinel NOT unlinked -> single sentinel authorizes multiple Writes within session TTL. Security gap introduced by this cycle's posttool Write branch. (2) AC4 fails — close debate requires clean test suite; environment contamination does not excuse the failing assertion. Codex also raised MAJOR: AC3 missing non_zero terminal case and session-keyed posttool mismatch test. Codex OBSERVATION_ONLY: 3-candidate lookup is correct (no shadowing, de-dup handles CLAUDE_TASK_ID==session_id case correctly). Codex noted pre-existing load_sentinel_grant_for_task duplicate-shadow bug (mtime sort picks expired nonce) but rated it out-of-scope for this cycle.

checks.name: Posttool Write sentinel consumption — task_id mismatch probe
checks.detail: Live probe: sentinel written keyed by session_id 'test-probe-session', posttool invoked with task_id='different-task-id'. posttool resolves task_id as data.task_id ('different-task-id'), calls load_sentinel_grant_for_task('different-task-id') -> None. Sentinel NOT found, NOT consumed. Sentinel file persists on disk. Additional Write event with same session_id + different task_id -> same result -> sentinel still persists. Sentinel authorized two Write events without being consumed. This is a regression introduced by this cycle: the pretool uses a 3-candidate lookup (Fix 1) but the posttool Write branch uses a single lookup, creating an asymmetry.

checks.name: Fix 3: AC3 Bash-with-Write-sentinel hard assertion
checks.detail: test_AC3.py line 115: assert os.path.exists(sentinel_path), 'Write sentinel must NOT be consumed by a Bash PostToolUse event'. Confirmed in source. Iter1 softened assertion replaced with hard assert. Case verifies posttool does not cross-consume Write sentinels during Bash PostToolUse events.

checks.name: CF-1 invariant — existing-file-only guard
checks.detail: write-guard.sh line 94: CF-1 denial only fires when _WG_SENTINEL_RESULT='valid_no_match' AND [ -f FILE_PATH ] (existing file). New-file creation (non-existing) bypasses CF-1. Subprocess verified: new file + mismatched sentinel -> exit 0. Existing file + mismatched sentinel -> exit 2. Existing file + targetless sentinel -> exit 0 (wildcard match, Fix 2 applies). All 3 CF-1 cases PASS.

blockers.description: Posttool task_id mismatch — Write sentinel not consumed on PostToolUse event when sentinel key != data.task_id
blockers.detail: posttool-allowlist-consume.py Write branch resolves task_id as data.task_id || CLAUDE_TASK_ID || session_id (no candidate list). The pretool uses a 3-candidate list [env_CLAUDE_TASK_ID or session_id, data.task_id, session_id] to handle the case where userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh keys sentinels by CLAUDE_TASK_ID:-SID (often session_id). The posttool Write branch does not apply this same resolution, so when sentinel_key=session_id and PostToolUse event carries data.task_id!=session_id, the sentinel is never consumed on-event. Confirmed live: sentinel persisted across 2 separate Write events. A single sentinel can authorize unlimited Writes within the session TTL window (default 300s). Dev marked this out_of_scope per dispatch DO NOT touch list, but posttool-allowlist-consume.py IS in dev.files_modified and the Write branch was added this cycle. The asymmetry between pretool (3-candidate) and posttool (1-candidate) is a cycle-introduced architectural gap, not a pre-existing condition.
blockers.recommendation: Apply the same 3-candidate lookup logic to the posttool Write branch: build [env_CLAUDE_TASK_ID or session_id, data.task_id, session_id] de-duped list, try match_sentinel_grant_for_write() against each candidate, consume on first match.

blockers.description: AC4 scope-integrity test fails — blocks clean pytest run required for close
blockers.detail: AC4 asserts that hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh does NOT appear in git diff --name-only HEAD. Sibling task 20260521-090200 has uncommitted changes to this file in the working tree, causing AC4 to fail. Not caused by this task's dev. However close debate requires all active tests to pass (or be waived with explicit documentation). AC4 is a binding acceptance criterion and cannot be silently skipped due to environment state. The working tree must be clean of sibling contamination before QA can issue a PASS verdict.
blockers.recommendation: Stash or commit sibling task 20260521-090200 changes before rerunning QA, OR explicitly waive AC4 via spec-check.py waive with documented rationale that the contamination is environment-scoped.

observations.description: Grant writer (userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh) emits args_contain not target for '/allow Write /path'
observations.detail: When user invokes '/allow Write /specific/path', userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh writes {op: 'Write', args_contain: ['/specific/path']} NOT {op: 'Write', target: '/specific/path'}. match_sentinel_grant_for_write() checks only 'target' field, not 'args_contain'. So '/allow Write /path' becomes a wildcard grant (target absent = any path). This is a WN1-scoped limitation documented in the BA spec. No path to exploit it without physical session access. Out-of-scope for this cycle per BA spec WN1.

observations.description: AC3 missing non_zero terminal-result case for posttool sentinel consumption
observations.detail: test_AC3 tests Write-success and Write-failure (is_error=True) consumption. Missing case: tool_response={exit_code: 1} (non_zero) — confirm sentinel consumed on non-zero exit. consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result() is designed to consume on any terminal result, but this case is not tested. Minor coverage gap.

When multiple JSON files exist for the same task_id prefix (e.g., task_id.json and task_id-nonce.json), load_sentinel_grant_for_task() sorts by mtime and takes the newest. An expired nonce with a newer mtime shadows a valid older grant, returning None when a valid grant exists. Pre-existing bug, out-of-scope for this cycle. No active tests create this condition. Codex confirmed live with a synthetic expired-shadow test.

iter2_fixes_verified.fix1_3candidate_lookup: PASS — pretool correctly resolves sentinel by writer-aligned candidate list, iter1 task_id mismatch resolved
iter2_fixes_verified.fix2_targetless_wildcard: PASS — match_sentinel_grant_for_write() returns entry when target absent or None, iter1 targetless-grant CF-1 regression resolved
iter2_fixes_verified.fix3_ac3_hard_assertion: PASS — Bash-with-Write-sentinel case now has hard os.path.exists() assert, sentinel isolation confirmed

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 2 BLOCKERs with PROPOSED_FIX: (1) posttool task_id mismatch is a security gap enabling multi-use sentinels within TTL window — fix by applying 3-candidate lookup to posttool Write branch; (2) PASS verdict cannot stand while AC4 explicitly fails, regardless of environment cause — fix by cleaning working tree before rerunning QA. 4 MAJORs and 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY also raised. Codex confirmed 3-candidate lookup is logically correct (OBSERVATION_ONLY). Codex confirmed pre-existing load_sentinel duplicate-shadow bug is out-of-scope.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both BLOCKERs adopted as blockers B1 and B2 in this report. Verdict changed from draft PASS to FAIL. Posttool mismatch reclassified from out_of_scope to critical/dev_implementation because posttool was modified this cycle and the posttool Write branch introduced the asymmetry. MAJOR findings OBS-1 through OBS-3 documented as observations (non-blocking per codex guidance: no PROPOSED_FIX required for OBSERVATION_ONLY, and MAJORs without security-critical impact do not block independently given the two critical BLOCKERs already present).

qa.overall_assessment: Iter2 fixes are correctly implemented and live-verified PASS. But two critical blockers prevent closing: (1) posttool Write branch lacks the 3-candidate task_id lookup that pretool has, creating a single-sentinel multi-Write security gap; (2) AC4 test fails due to environment contamination. Recommend: fix posttool Write branch to use candidate list, clean sibling contamination from working tree, then re-run QA.

success_criteria_results.criterion: AC1: match_sentinel_grant_for_write() exists in allowlist.py and matches/rejects correctly
success_criteria_results.evidence: Function at allowlist.py:484, 40/40 consolidation tests pass including 5 TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite cases, AC1 test 4/4 PASS

success_criteria_results.criterion: AC2: pretool-write-guard.sh checks sentinel before legacy /allow grant
success_criteria_results.evidence: Sentinel check at write-guard.sh:46-96 before legacy /allow block at line 97. CF-1 invariant verified via subprocess. 4/4 AC2 smoke cases PASS.

success_criteria_results.criterion: AC3: posttool-allowlist-consume.py consumes Write sentinel on terminal result
success_criteria_results.evidence: elif tool_name=='Write' branch at posttool:140-166. AC3 3/3 cases PASS. Bash event does NOT consume Write sentinel (hard assert). NOTE: consumption only works when data.task_id matches sentinel key — see blocker B1.

success_criteria_results.criterion: AC4: scope integrity — no files outside hooks/ modified
success_criteria_results.evidence: AC4 test asserts userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh absent from git diff HEAD. File appears due to sibling task 20260521-090200 environment contamination. Test fails. primary_cause: environment.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The three primary root causes from iter1 (absent match_sentinel_grant_for_write(), absent sentinel check in write-guard, posttool restricted to Bash only) are all addressed. The iter1 critical failures (task_id mismatch, targetless wildcard regression) are both fixed. A new architectural gap was introduced: posttool Write branch uses single-candidate lookup while pretool uses 3-candidate, creating an asymmetry that enables multi-use sentinels.
